input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers/linkers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI (`dotnet`) |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by the MAUI workload, or manually via [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |

## 2. Installation Steps (in order)

```bash
# ① Install Xcode from the App Store, then accept the license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer

# ② Install .NET SDK (download the macOS installer from dot.net/download)
#    After install, verify:
dotnet --version

# ③ Install the MAUI workload (handles Android SDK, JDK, emulator):
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# ④ (Optional) Install Android device/emulator images:
#    The workload installs the Android SDK. To manage emulators:
#    Use the sdkmanager bundled at:
#    ~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager
```

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# List available .NET SDKs and runtimes:
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet --list-runtimes

# See installed workloads & check for updates:
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# Check which MAUI version maps to your SDK:
dotnet workload search maui

# Check Xcode & iOS SDK version:
xcodebuild -version
xcrun --sdk iphoneos --show-sdk-version
```

**Version guidance:** Use the latest **.NET 8 (LTS)** or **.NET 9** SDK. The `maui` workload version is pinned to your SDK band — `dotnet workload install maui` always pulls the correct match.

## 4. Create & Run a Test Project

```bash
# Create from template:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for all targets:
dotnet build

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios
#   (or net9.0-ios, matching your SDK)

# Run on Android Emulator:
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 5. Verification Checklist

| Check | Command | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| .NET installed | `dotnet --version` | `8.x.x` or `9.x.x` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` | `maui` listed & up-to-date |
| Xcode CLI | `xcode-select -p` | Path to Xcode Developer dir |
| iOS SDK | `xcrun --sdk iphoneos --show-sdk-version` | e.g. `17.x` or `18.x` |
| Android SDK | `~/.../sdkmanager --list` | Platforms & build-tools listed |
| Template builds | `dotnet build` in a `maui` project | Build succeeded |
| iOS sim runs | `dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios` | App launches in Simulator |
| Android emu runs | `dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android` | App launches in Emulator |

## 6. Optional but Recommended

- **IDE:** [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + the [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui), or [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/).
- **Hot Reload:** Supported out-of-the-box with `dotnet watch` or VS Code.
- **Physical iOS device:** Requires an Apple Developer account and provisioning profile configured in Xcode.

All sources are official Microsoft/Apple channels. The key insight is that `dotnet workload install maui` handles the Android SDK, build tools, and JDK for you — Xcode is the only major manual install.
